layman_headline: Market fear and geopolitical tensions make this a poor time to swing into altcoins.
layman_explanation: The crypto market is pulling back due to renewed U.S.-Iran tensions near the Strait of Hormuz, with Bitcoin dropping below $63,000. The overall mood is fearful (28/100), and Bitcoin dominance at 56% means money is flowing away from altcoins into Bitcoin for safety. Every swing trade candidate on the watchlist has significant problems — either already pumped too much, has bearish chart structure, or lacks a clear risk/reward setup. This is a time to wait, not chase.
layman_action: Stay in cash and wait for the geopolitical dust to settle. Better entry points will come when fear peaks or a clear reversal pattern forms. Don't force a trade in a falling market.
expert_reasoning: I evaluated all 9 candidates systematically. DCR has the most compelling single signal — RSI at 19.6 is deeply oversold (historically reliable per our signal credibility data). However, it already bounced 25% in 7 days and its chart bias is bearish (-0.7) with price below all MAs. This looks like a dead cat bounce, not a sustainable reversal. DEXE is RSI 77 and up 57% in a week — chasing this is reckless. ETHFI has the best chart bias (+0.6) but risk/reward is terrible at 0.11x with resistance at $0.40 right overhead. GRASS dropped 21% in 7 days but shows a bearish engulfing pattern with -0.9 chart bias — catching this knife has no technical support. The macro backdrop — falling BTC, high BTC dominance squeezing alts, geopolitical risk from Hormuz — argues strongly for patience. Fear & Greed improving from 24.6 weekly average to 28 is a modest positive, but not enough to override the bearish setup across the board.
risk_factors: U.S.-Iran Hormuz tensions could escalate further, triggering a broader risk-off selloff across crypto
BTC dominance at 56% continues to drain liquidity from altcoins, making alt swing trades especially risky
No candidate offers risk/reward above 1.5x except KITE (6x R/R but deeply bearish chart structure)
watch_list: BTC price action around $60,000 support — a break below could trigger a cascade of alt liquidations
Resolution or escalation of U.S.-Iran Hormuz tensions over the next 48 hours
DCR price behavior near $10 support — if it holds and RSI stays oversold, a proper bounce entry may form
market_context: Bitcoin fell below $63,000 on geopolitical risk from U.S.-Iran hostilities near the Strait of Hormuz, despite positive institutional signals (BlackRock/Goldman joining UK tokenization taskforce, strong ETF flows). High BTC dominance at 56% is crushing altcoin performance, making this a dangerous environment for alt swing trades.
context_banner: Geopolitical headwinds and 56% BTC dominance create a hostile environment for altcoin swing trades — capital preservation is the priority.

key_signals.description: Bitcoin fell below $63K as markets assign just 3% probability of Hormuz shipping normalization by August, signaling sustained geopolitical risk premium.
Fear & Greed at 28 (Fear) with improving trend from 24.6 weekly average — sentiment is recovering but not yet at extreme fear levels that historically mark bottoms.
DCR RSI at 19.6 is deeply oversold (strongest oversold reading on watchlist), but the 25% 7-day rally suggests the easy bounce has already happened.
Profit-taking combined with Middle East hostilities dragging crypto lower after a bullish week — confirms this is a risk-off rotation, not an entry opportunity.
BTC bull market restart projected for September per analysts — suggests patience is warranted and better entries lie ahead.
